steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.vocare: 'vocare' is an infinitive (to call), not a finite verb

hints.present indicative: Present 1st person would be 'venio' — 'veni' is perfect
hints.imperative: Imperative would be 'veni' (same form!), but context shows 'I came' not 'come!'
hints.imperfect: Imperfect would be 'veniebam' — 'veni' is perfect indicative

hints.vendo, vendere, vendidi, venditum: These are principal parts of 'vendo' (to sell)
hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are principal parts of 'vinco' (to conquer)
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are principal parts of 'video' (to see)

hints.imperative: Imperative would be 'voca' — 'vocare' ending in -are is the infinitive
hints.participle: Participle would be 'vocans' or 'vocatus' — 'vocare' is the infinitive
hints.gerund: Gerund would be 'vocandi' — 'vocare' is the present active infinitive

hints.to devote: 'to devote' is 'devovere/vocare' can overlap, but 'vocare' means 'to call'

hints.volo, volare, volavi, volatum: These are principal parts of 'volo' (to fly)
hints.voveo, vovere, vovi, votum: These are principal parts of 'voveo' (to vow)
hints.volvo, volvere, volvi, volutum: These are principal parts of 'volvo' (to roll)

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'iusti' — 'iustos' with -os ending is accusative plural
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'iustis' — 'iustos' is accusative plural
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'iustorum' — 'iustos' is accusative plural

translation: I did not come to call the righteous
